original: Arsenius asks for the way of salvation and receives the words: flee, be silent, pray always.
english: The path narrows into three movements: withdrawal, silence, and prayer.
spanish: El camino se estrecha en tres movimientos: retiro, silencio y oración.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 1–2
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 8. Short working rendering.

original: Arsenius prays that, though he has done nothing good, he may now begin.
english: The beginning of good can start after a lifetime of not yet having begun.
spanish: El comienzo del bien puede empezar después de una vida entera sin haber comenzado.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 3
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 8. Short working rendering.

original: The educated Arsenius says he does not know even the alphabet of the Egyptian peasant.
english: A library can still kneel before an alphabet learned by practice.
spanish: Una biblioteca todavía puede arrodillarse ante un alfabeto aprendido por práctica.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 6
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 8. Short working rendering.

original: Arsenius tells a brother to bring his interior activity into accord with God.
english: Outer passions weaken when the inner work is aligned before them.
spanish: Las pasiones exteriores se debilitan cuando el trabajo interior se alinea primero.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 9
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 9. Short working rendering.

artifact: Arsenius 11 — Do Not Leave the Cell
original: Arsenius answers troubled thoughts by saying: eat, drink, sleep, do no work, only do not leave the cell.
english: When discipline collapses, keep the smallest boundary that still saves attention.
spanish: Cuando la disciplina colapsa, conserva el límite mínimo que todavía salva la atención.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 11
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 9. Short working rendering.

original: Arsenius says the heavenly hosts have one will, while human beings have many.
english: Solitude protects the single will from being scattered into a crowd of wills.
spanish: La soledad protege la voluntad única de dispersarse en una multitud de voluntades.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 13
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 9. Short working rendering.

original: Arsenius grieves when he is not given a share of a small common blessing.
english: Humility receives even the smallest gift as a place at the common table.
spanish: La humildad recibe hasta el don más pequeño como un lugar en la mesa común.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 16
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 9. Short working rendering.

original: Arsenius hears reeds moving in the wind and speaks of the fragility of silent prayer.
english: When silence is deep, even reeds in the wind become a storm.
spanish: Cuando el silencio es profundo, hasta los juncos en el viento se vuelven tormenta.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 25
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 11. Short working rendering.

artifact: Arsenius 29 — Already Dead to the Will
original: Arsenius refuses a large inheritance, saying he was dead before the donor died.
english: What has already died to ownership cannot inherit the world again.
spanish: Lo que ya murió a la propiedad no puede heredar el mundo otra vez.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 29
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 12. Short working rendering.

original: Arsenius turns toward prayer until the sun shines again on his face.
english: The night is measured by a face waiting for light to return.
spanish: La noche se mide por un rostro que espera el regreso de la luz.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 30
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 12. Short working rendering.

original: Arsenius sees images of labor made vain by pride, leakage, and refusal to yield.
english: Labor becomes empty when pride will not turn the burden lengthwise through the door.
spanish: El trabajo se vacía cuando el orgullo no gira la carga para pasar por la puerta.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 33
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, pp. 13-14. Short working rendering.

original: Arsenius and Moses appear in two boats: one in peace, the other in hospitality.
english: Withdrawal and welcome can both be holy when each serves the same name.
spanish: El retiro y la acogida pueden ser santos cuando ambos sirven al mismo nombre.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 38
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 15. Short working rendering.

original: Arsenius says he has often repented of speaking, but never of being silent.
english: Speech often asks forgiveness; silence rarely does.
spanish: La palabra pide perdón a menudo; el silencio casi nunca.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 40
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 15. Short working rendering.

original: Poemen blesses Arsenius for weeping for himself in this world.
english: Tears are inevitable; wisdom chooses the ones that can still change a life.
spanish: Las lágrimas son inevitables; la sabiduría elige las que aún pueden cambiar una vida.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 41
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 16. Short working rendering.

original: Arsenius compares the shown soul to a daughter no longer hidden in her father's house.
english: Once the hidden life is displayed, it must answer to many eyes instead of one gaze.
spanish: Cuando la vida escondida se exhibe, responde a muchos ojos y no a una sola mirada.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Arsenius 44
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 17. Short working rendering.
